## Liver Cancer Patient Queries — Diagnosis + Elevated AFP

### 🔵 Query 1 — Coded HCC / Primary Liver Cancer (ICD-10-CM)
Covers the full C22.x block: hepatocellular carcinoma, intrahepatic bile duct CA, hepatoblastoma, angiosarcoma, and unspecified primary liver malignancies.

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C22.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C22.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C22.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C22.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C22.4,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C22.7,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C22.8,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C22.9&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### 🔵 Query 2 — Coded HCC / Liver Cancer (SNOMED CT)
Catches SNOMED-coded diagnoses that may not carry an ICD-10 code (e.g., problem-list entries).

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|109841003,http://snomed.info/sct|25370001,http://snomed.info/sct|187757009&_include=Condition:subject
```

| SNOMED Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `109841003` | Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) |
| `25370001` | Carcinoma of liver |
| `187757009` | Malignant neoplasm of liver & intrahepatic bile ducts |

---

### 🔴 Query 3 — Elevated AFP (≥ 20 ng/mL) — *Undiagnosed / Surveillance Cases*
Threshold ≥ 20 ng/mL = above ULN (AASLD guideline); ≥ 400 ng/mL = highly suggestive of HCC. The `ge20` filter captures both.

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|1834-1,http://loinc.org|19176-4,http://loinc.org|83086-6&value-quantity=ge20|http://unitsofmeasure.org|ng/mL&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

| LOINC Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `1834-1` | AFP \[Mass/vol\] in Serum or Plasma *(most common)* |
| `19176-4` | AFP Cancer Ag \[Units/vol\] in Serum or Plasma |
| `83086-6` | AFP \[Mass/vol\] by Immunoassay |

---

### 🔗 Union Logic (Post-Processing)

```
UNION(
  Condition results [Query 1 — ICD-10],
  Condition results [Query 2 — SNOMED],
  Observation.subject results [Query 3 — AFP ≥ 20]
)
DEDUPLICATE by Patient.id
```

> **Key notes:**
> - Queries 1 & 2 should be **intersected with `clinical-status=active`** if you only want current diagnoses vs. historical
> - For AFP, consider adding a **date filter** (`date=ge2025-01-01`) to avoid surfacing long-resolved elevations
> - Patients appearing **only in Query 3** are your highest-yield undiagnosed/uncodified cohort for prospective workup
